# UC-13.7.3 · Fluentd Input-to-Output Throughput Mismatch Detection

## Description

Sustained divergence between ingress and egress counters means filters are dropping unexpectedly, buffers are stalling, or metrics scraping is incomplete. The gap metric catches subtle pipeline starvation before disk buffers fill.

## Value

Protects log completeness SLAs and accelerates root cause during partial outages.

## Implementation

Use `eventstats` to pivot in→out per host if `xyseries` is awkward: `stats sum(eval(if(dir="in",vol,null))) as vin ...` then `eval gap=abs(vin-vout)`. Align poll intervals; both legs must share timestamps.

## SPL

```spl
index=fluent sourcetype="fluentd:plugins"
| eval vol=coalesce(emit_records, emit_count, 0)
| bin _time span=1m
| stats sum(eval(if(match(plugin_type,"^in"),vol,0))) as vin, sum(eval(if(match(plugin_type,"^out"),vol,0))) as vout by _time, host
| eval gap=abs(vin-vout)
| where vin > 1000 AND gap > vin * 0.25
| sort -gap
```

## Visualization

Dual-axis timechart (in vs out), gap histogram, table of worst hosts.

## References

- [Fluentd — Monitoring Fluentd](https://docs.fluentd.org/deployment/monitoring-fluentd)
- [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)
